rulings.Case Title: LOUIS GAMACHE et al VS. NICK VOLENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amtech'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint, Motion To Strike And Motion To Sever
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 6. Amtech'S Demurrer To First Amended Cross-Complaint, Motion To Strike And Motion To Sever Cross-Defendant Amtech's Motion to Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint Execution is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Cross-Complainant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Court's order on Motion for Judgment on the pleadings, which is limited in scope to the pleadings before the Court at the time of the motion, allows addition of new causes of action in the amended complaint pursuant to that order. Therefore, Motion to Strike is DENIED as to causes of action 2 (comparative indemnity) and 4 (contribution) as it can be ascertained from the sole cause of action in Cross-Complaint that Cross-complainants attempted to allege such causes of action. See 6. GRANTED as to other causes of action as they were not previously alleged in the sole cause of action and no leave to amend was granted, they also do not arise for the same facts/do not relate back as Cross-Complaint was devoid of any facts. Cross-Defendant Amtech's Demurrer to First Amended Cross-Complaint is Moot in part and SUSTAINED with leave to amend in part. Demurrer is moot as to causes of action 3, 5, 6. Sustained with leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 4th. There are no factual allegations contained in the FACC. Allegations from Fourth Amended Complaint incorporated by reference and without any limitations make it impossible to ascertain for which acts Defendants seek indemnity and how most of those acts relate to Cross-Defnednat. E.g. See Fourth Amended Complaint 41, 44, 64, 66. Cross-Defendant Amtech's Motion to Sever and Vacate Trial date as Cross-Complaint only is granted. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MARY SHELBY VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 1. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to address all issues raised in demurrer. Specifically applicability of CCC 2923.5, facts showing loss of money or property, etc. Late-filed opposition is stricken. C. Scott Greene, Andrea M. Hicks, Thomas S. Lee, Jessica T. Ehsanian and Bryan Cave, LLP are ordered to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6C in future. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: 1320-1380 LOMBARD STREET, LLC VS. LETICIA GONZALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Req. For Admissions, Set One, Req. For Production Of Documents, Set One, Special Interrogatories; Etc
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT LETICIA GONZALES's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Req. For Admissions, Set One, Req. For Production Of Documents, Set One, Special Interrogatories Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet-and confer efforts. Defendant did not follow up on her discovery after she failed to file timely response resulting in default. Plaintiff failed to provide its response to Separate Statement, rendering its position on reasons for further responses" unclear. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: 945 LARKIN STREET, LLC VS. LUIS A. HEATH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Luis Heath'S Demurrer To Complaint Per Order Of The Court
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 3. Defendant Luis Heath's Demurrer To Complaint OVERRULED, 5 days to answer; Unlawful Detainer based on termination of license is sufficiently alleged and written contract is attached. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: TUAN LE VS. DAVID LAWSON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT JOSEPH LAWSON's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Defendant fails to indicate any portion of the Complaint subject to motion to strike. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID LEATH et al VS. EBONEE CRAWFORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 8, 2013, line 5. PLAINTIFF DAVID LEATH, LILA LEATH's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. Sanctions granted in the amount of $610. = (501/AJR)
